data.title: UK's GDP misses market expectation, trade deficit widens
data.description:
• UK's GDP remained unchanged M/M at -0.10% in October, below estimates of 0.10%.

  • Monthly GDP YoY in the United Kingdom remained unchanged at 1.10% in October, lower than estimates of 1.4%.

  • Manufacturing production increased to 0.50% M/M in October from -1.70% in September, while industrial production decreased 0.80% over the same month in the previous year. 

  • The total goods and services trade deficit widened by £4.0 billion to £6.7 billion in the three months to October 2025. Exports of goods to the United States, including precious metals, rose by £1.1 billion (27%) in October 2025, while imports of goods rose by £2.2 billion (51.6%).

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Halliburton, VoltaGrid lock in 400 MW of gas power systems for 2028 data center build-out
data.description:
Halliburton (HAL) and VoltaGrid have secured manufacturing for 400 megawatts (MW) of modular natural gas power systems, scheduled for delivery in 2028, to support the development of data centers across the Eastern Hemisphere, the companies announced on Thursday.

The collaboration combines Halliburton’s (HAL) operational expertise and global footprint with VoltaGrid’s distributed power platform to deliver reliable, scalable, and efficient power for hyperscale data centers, critical to supporting artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and digital transformation.

“The Eastern Hemisphere represents a transformational opportunity for data center investment and associated power generation. We are excited about collaborating with Halliburton and making our first capital commitment to the region," Nathan Ough, VoltaGrid CEO, said.

data.title: CenterPoint raises dividend by 5% to $0.23
data.description:
• CenterPoint (CNP) declares $0.23/share quarterly dividend, 4.5% increase from prior dividend of $0.22.
  • Forward yield 2.46%

data.title: Contineum Therapeutics stock drops as company prices upsized $90 million offering
data.description:
• Contineum Therapeutics (CTNM) late on Thursday announced the pricing of an upsized underwritten public offering of 7,346,938 shares of its Class A common stock at a public offering price of $12.25 per share. 
  • Contineum Therapeutics (CTNM) shares fell 3.3% in extended trading. 
  • In addition, Contineum has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1,102,040 shares of its Class A common stock at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering, before deducting underwriting discounts and commissions and other estimated offering expenses payable by Contineum, are expected to be approximately $90.0 million.
  • The offering is expected to close December 15.

data.title: Taiwan unveils cloud center powered by Nvidia chips
data.description:
Taiwan President Lai Ching-te on Friday opened a new cloud computing hub in Tainan, home to the 15-megawatt “Nano 4” supercomputer built with Nvidia chips.

The government wants the new cloud facility in the southern city of Tainan as a central driver of artificial intelligence development and a hub for innovation in high-performance computing, telecommunications, cloud services, and digital content.

The 15-megawatt facility hosts the "Nano 4" supercomputer, the largest and latest supercomputer in Taiwan. It is equipped with 1,760 of Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 chips and 144 of its Blackwell chips, Reuters reported, citing Taiwan's National Science and Technology Council.

U.S. chip designer Nvidia's main chip supplier is Taiwan's TSMC (TSM), the world's largest contract chipmaker and the backbone of the island's economy.

data.title: SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son considers buying data center operator Switch
data.description:
SoftBank Group (SFTBY) (SFTBF), led by Masayoshi Son, is exploring the acquisition of U.S. data center operator Switch that aligns with his aggressive push into AI, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Switch specializes in energy-efficient data centers essential for AI workloads. Acquiring Switch would provide SoftBank (SFTBY) outright ownership of a major portfolio at a time when demand for computing power is growing.

Switch's owners are aiming for a valuation of about $50 billion, including debt, the report added. The potential deal signals SoftBank's (SFTBY) shift toward owning the full AI stack.

The parties have not reached an agreement on the terms of a deal, and there’s no certainty the discussions will lead to a transaction, the people said.

data.title: Legence selling stockholders prices $378.1M upsized secondary offering at $45.00 a share
data.description:
Legence (LGN) announced the pricing of an upsized secondary public offering of its Class A common stock, that includes 8,402,178 shares sold by certain stockholders affiliated with Blackstone Inc. (the "Selling Stockholders") at a price of $45.00 per share.

The offering is valued at approximately $378.1 million for the firm term.

The secondary offering was upsized from the initially announced 7 million shares to be sold by Blackstone-affiliated stockholders.

The selling stockholders also granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1,260,326 shares.

Legence is not selling any shares in this offering and will not receive any proceeds from the sale.

The transaction is expected to close on or about December 16, 2025.
